title: Small Crabs Do a Big Cleanup

content.en: Mangrove forests grow where the land and the sea meet. They are muddy!
content.tw: 紅樹林生長在陸地與海的交界處，那裡都是泥巴地。

content.en: In Colombia, the mud in a mangrove forest is dirty. It has many very small pieces of plastic in it. But, fiddler crabs are very busy there!
content.tw: 在哥倫比亞的一片紅樹林裡面，地上的泥巴藏了很多小塑膠碎片跟忙個不停的招潮蟹。

content.en: Maybe you have seen fiddler crabs in Taiwan. They are about three to five centimeters wide. Male crabs have one big claw and one small claw. Female crabs have two small claws. They live in mud. Every day, they eat mud.
content.tw: 你也許在台灣也見過招潮蟹！牠們只有大約三到五公分寬。公的招潮蟹有一隻大螯跟一隻小螯，母蟹則有兩隻小螯。招潮蟹躲在泥巴裡，每天都吃泥巴裡的微小生物。

content.en: Scientists studied some fiddler crabs in Colombia. When these crabs eat mud, they eat plastic too. This cleans the mud. There is more plastic in the crabs than in the mud!
content.tw: 科學家研究這些哥倫比亞的招潮蟹，發現牠們在吃泥巴的時候也會把塑膠一起吃進去，讓泥巴變乾淨了。

content.en: The crabs' stomach breaks plastic into smaller pieces. It does this in a few days. It does this faster than sunlight! It does this faster than ocean waves too! But too much plastic is bad for the crabs.
content.tw: 招潮蟹的胃可以把塑膠分解成更小的碎片，而且速度比用陽光或海浪分解快很多。不過，吃太多塑膠對螃蟹還是不好！

content.en: These small crabs are helping us. But they need our help too. Please recycle plastic. If you cannot, then throw it away in the right place!
content.tw: 這些小螃蟹正在幫助我們，但牠們也需要我們的幫助。請回收塑膠。如果你不能回收，請把它丟在正確的地方！

vocabulary.preface: 科學家發現招潮蟹有清潔環境的功能，並且效率高。

content.text:
1. big 大的。
What are these little creatures? 這些小東西是什麼啊? They look funny! 看起來很好玩! They are fiddler crabs. 是招潮蟹。See, one claw is very big, the other is small. 你看，一隻螯很大，一隻很小。

2. smaller 比較小的。
Even the body is smaller than the claw. 連身體都比它的螯小。Why? 為什麼? These are male crabs. 這些是公蟹。They use the big claws to fight. 牠們用大螯來戰鬥。

3. land 陸地。
Can they swim if I throw them into the sea? 如果丟進海裡，牠們會游泳嗎? No, don't do that. 不會，不要那樣。They live on land. 牠們住在陸地上。

4. mud 泥巴。
And they eat mud. Yuck! 好難吃。 I know. But they like it. 我知道，不過牠們喜歡。

vocabulary.postscript: 今天的單字你記住了嗎? big大的 smaller比較小的 land陸地 mud泥巴

quiz.question: 1. What kind of claws do male fiddler crabs have?
options: a. One small claw, one big claw

quiz.question: 2. What has more plastic?
3. What happens to the plastic inside the crabs?
options: b. It breaks into smaller pieces.

title: Some Warm Clothes Can Make Kids Itch

content.en: The weather is getting warmer all over Taiwan. But sometimes it is cold. What do you wear then? Hmmmm. Clothes with fleece inside are soft and warm…
content.tw: 天氣變涼的時候，穿有刷毛的衣服既柔軟又保暖！

content.en: But wearing fleece can cause problems for kids. They can get red, itchy skin! This is not comfortable. For some kids, it hurts!
content.tw: 可是，刷毛的衣服有時會讓小朋友的皮膚很不舒服！他們的皮膚會變紅、發癢，甚至會痛。

content.en: Moms and dads were worried. Why was their kid's skin itchy? Some of them took their kid to the doctor.
content.tw: 很多爸爸媽媽很擔心！為了知道小朋友皮膚癢的原因，他們還帶孩子去看醫生。

content.en: Food was not making these kids itch. Bug bites were not making them itch. Doctors said fleece clothes were making kids itch!
content.tw: 原來，小朋友皮膚癢不是因為食物過敏或是被昆蟲咬，而是因為穿了刷毛的衣服！

content.en: When kids run and play, their clothes rub their skin. When they wear fleece next to their skin, kids can get hot and itchy. Kids also sweat. This makes their itchy skin itch more!
content.tw: 當小朋友奔跑、玩耍的時候，衣服會摩擦他們的皮膚。穿刷毛的衣服時，皮膚就會被摩擦的又紅又癢！如果小朋友流汗的話，那就更癢了！

content.en: So when the weather is cold, what can you do? Doctors say wearing 100% cotton clothes is the best. Wear them next to your skin. Then you can wear fleece or other warm clothes on top of your cotton clothes.
content.tw: 那麼天氣冷的時候到底該怎麼穿呢？醫生建議，可以在最裡面穿一件100%棉質的衣服，再套刷毛或其他保暖的衣服！

content.en: You will be warm this way. And your skin will not itch!
content.tw: 這樣穿既保暖，你的皮膚也不會癢了！

vocabulary.preface: 有些保暖的衣物，可能會引起兒童皮膚過敏。

content.text:
1. weather 天氣。
Wearing a sweater today was a bad idea. 今天穿毛衣真糟糕! It's way too warm! 太熱了!
Yeah, the weather's messing with you. 你被天氣給耍了。

2. itch 發癢。
This morning was cold, and now I'm melting and itching!
今天早上還很冷，現在我都要融化了，還很癢!
You'd better lose that sweater. 你最好脫掉那件毛衣。

3. cotton 棉花。
I've got a cotton shirt in my bag. 我包包裡有棉襯杉。
You can borrow it if you don't mind. 可以借你，如果你不介意的話。

4. best 最好的。
Is it clean? 乾淨嗎?
It smells okay. 聞起來還好。
That's not very convincing… 聽起來不大有說服力。
But yeah, I guess that's the best I can get right now. 不過好吧，現在最好也只能這樣。

vocabulary.postscript:
今天的單字你記住了嗎?
weather天氣 itch發癢 cotton棉花 best最好的

quiz.question: 1. What made kids' skin itchy?
2. What makes itchy skin itch more?
3. What should you wear next to your skin?

title: Man Wants Big Home for Birds

content.en: In Kenya, a boy lived near a lake in a big park. Many birds lived there too.
content.tw: 在肯亞，有個小男孩住在湖邊，這個湖在大公園裡。

content.en: The boy liked watching the birds. Sometimes a bird was hurt, sick, or hungry. He tried to help them.
content.tw: 湖邊有很多鳥，小男孩喜歡看這些鳥。當發現受傷、生病、或是肚子餓的鳥，他總是想辦法幫忙。

content.en: The boy grew up. He went to live in Nairobi. Nairobi is a loud, busy city. Cars drive fast. Buildings have many windows. And power lines are everywhere.
content.tw: 小男孩長大後，搬去了奈洛比。奈洛比是個吵雜忙碌的城市，路上的車子開得很快，建築物有很多窗戶，並且到處都是電線。

content.en: The young man often saw hurt birds. They hit windows. They hit power lines. But no one helped them.
content.tw: 這名男子常常看到受傷的鳥。有的鳥會撞到窗戶或是電線，可是都沒有人伸出援手。

content.en: So the man helps birds again. He feeds them. He cares for them. But his home is very small. He cannot keep the birds until they get better.
content.tw: 於是他又開始幫助這些鳥，照顧牠們，還餵牠們吃東西。不過他的家很小，沒辦法讓鳥住到完全康復。

content.en: The man often walks with a bird on his head or shoulders. People call him the Nairobi Birdman.
content.tw: 這名男子走在路上時，頭頂或肩膀上往往停著一隻鳥，所以大家都叫他「奈洛比鳥人」。

content.en: The man wants to take care of more birds. He wants to have a big home for them. It will be a safe place for them to get better.
content.tw: 這名男子希望照顧更多小鳥，也希望有比較大的家，可以讓鳥安全地休息、慢慢好起來！

content.en: With some help, maybe the Nairobi Birdman can have a big home for birds. Then this young man can help more birds!
content.tw: 在一些幫助下，或許奈洛比鳥人可以為鳥兒們建立一個大家園。這樣這位年輕人就能幫助更多鳥兒了！

vocabulary.preface: 喜歡鳥類的小男孩，長大之後仍然努力幫助他的鳥朋友。

content.text:
1. watch 注意看。
I can't go shopping this weekend. 我這周末不能去逛街。
I'm going birdwatching. 我要去賞鳥。
Where are you going? 去哪裡?
The Botanical Garden. 植物園。

2. city 城市。
In Taipei city? 在台北市嗎?
Yeah. 對。
Birds stay there even with so many people?! 人那麼多，鳥兒還待在那裏?
They love it! There's a pond and many trees. 牠們很喜歡呢!
那裏有池塘，有好多樹。

3. place 地方。
It's a perfect place for birds. 對鳥來說很完美。

4. feed 餵食。
Do you feed them? 你會餵牠們嗎?
Nope. They're better at finding food than I am. 不，牠們找食物可比我行。

vocabulary.postscript:
Have you ever gone birdwatching?
watch注意看 city城市 place地方 feed餵食

quiz.question: 1. Where did the boy live?
2. How do birds get hurt in the city?
3. What do people call the man?

title: Sheep Find Beautiful Snack

content.en: A farm in Nantou has many cherry trees. Every year, the trees have many pink flowers. They are called cherry blossoms. Spring is the best time to see these beautiful blossoms.
content.tw: 南投有一個農場有很多櫻花樹。春天是最適合賞櫻的季節，春天一到，樹上都會開滿粉紅色的櫻花。

content.en: But this year, the sheep on the farm have another plan. To them, the blossoms look like delicious snacks!
content.tw: 但是今年這座農場裡的羊群，認為櫻花看起來像好吃的點心！

content.en: The sheep walk around the farm. They take small bites of the cherry blossoms. They eat some blossoms here. They eat some blossoms there.
content.tw: 羊群在農場裡走動，邊走邊吃櫻花。這裡吃一點，那裡也吃一點。

content.en: Soon, many cherry blossoms are gone! The wind did not blow them away. The sheep ate them!
content.tw: 沒有多久，櫻花的數量就變少了！櫻花不是被風吹落，而是被羊群吃掉了！

content.en: These sheep eat the cherry blossoms they can reach first. But they are smart. They also jump to get higher ones. They try very hard! Each sheep tries to get the last blossom!
content.tw: 這些羊會先吃離牠們比較近的花朵，但是牠們很聰明，也會跳起來吃比較高的花。牠們很努力的跳啊跳，每隻羊都想吃到最後一朵花！

content.en: People go to the farm to see cherry blossoms. And this year, they go to see the sheep too! They watch these sheep jump and eat the blossoms. It is fun! They laugh and take pictures.
content.tw: 原本只想賞花的遊客現在也會來看這些羊！大家看到跳來跳去的羊，覺得很有趣，還會笑著幫牠們拍照。

content.en: Have you seen cherry blossoms this spring? If you have not, hurry up! Or some sheep might eat them first!
content.tw: 今年春天你去看過櫻花嗎？如果你還沒去，快點喔！不然有些羊可能會先把它們吃光！

vocabulary.preface: 人類喜歡賞花，但是在羊群的眼中，花兒卻是美味的食物。

content.text:
1. cherry blossom 櫻花。
Where do you usually go for cherry blossoms? 你通常去哪裡賞櫻?
Well, I like cherries, but the blossoms? 我愛吃櫻桃，但是櫻花?
Not so much. 就沒那麼喜歡了。

2. pink 粉紅的。
Don’t you think they’re beautiful? 你不覺得它很漂亮?
Okay, they are pretty. 好吧，是很美。
The pink ones are my favorite. 我最喜歡粉紅色的。

3. flower 花。
Then why not go see them? 那為什麼不去看?
No, I wouldn’t go out of my way just for flowers. 不去，我不想專程去賞花。

4. delicious 美味的。
You’re not a romantic person, are you? 你不是個浪漫的人，對吧?
I bake delicious cherry pies. 我會烤美味的櫻桃派。
Does that make me romantic? 這樣算不算浪漫?

vocabulary.postscript:
今天的單字很可愛。
cherry blossom櫻花 pink粉紅的 flower花 delicious美味的

quiz.question: 1. Where is the farm?
2. When is the best time to see cherry blossoms?
3. Why are many cherry blossoms gone on the farm?
options: b. The wind blew them away.

title: Free School Lunches in Taiwan

content.en: Narrator: What did you eat for lunch? Some people say: Auntie: You are what you eat. Narrator: Does that mean if you eat a sausage, you are a sausage?! No, no, no.
content.tw: 你今天午餐吃什麼呢？有些人說「吃什麼像什麼」，但這不代表我們吃香腸就會變成香腸了！

content.en: When we eat healthy food, we feel good. Healthy food is good for us. It’s good for our bodies and brains. It gives us more energy. That’s what people mean when they say: Auntie: You are what you eat! Narrator: Thanks, Auntie!
content.tw: 當我們吃健康食物，身體與大腦會獲得很多能量，整體就變得健康。這就是「吃什麼像什麼」！

content.en: Schools in Taiwan want to help kids eat healthy food. But it can cost a lot of money!
content.tw: 學校希望學生們多吃健康食物，但是那些食物可不便宜！

content.en: Some people think free school lunches can help. They are worried. They say some families do not have money for healthy food. They think schools should help students eat healthy food every day.
content.tw: 有些家庭負擔不起，因此有人認為免費的營養午餐，會很有幫助！他們覺得學校應該確保學生天天都能吃得健康。

content.en: But some people do not think free school lunches can help. They are worried too. They say some schools do not have money to buy healthy food. They think schools will give students food that is not healthy.
content.tw: 但是也有人擔心經費不足的學校，沒辦法供應學生營養午餐，於是就給學生吃一些便宜而不健康的東西。

content.en: Next year, most schools in Taiwan will have free school lunches. Will this help students eat healthy food? What do you think?
content.tw: 明年，台灣大部分的學校將會有免費的營養午餐。這會幫助學生吃得健康嗎？你覺得呢？

vocabulary.preface: 學校的營養午餐，對很多小朋友來說是重要的一餐。

content.text:
1. lunch 午餐。
What are we having for lunch? 我們中飯吃什麼?
Something healthy. 健康的東西。
You don’t mean vegetable soup and cheese, right?
你的意思不是指青菜湯跟起司吧?
Hey, they are healthy! 那些是很健康啊!

2. body 身體。
Maybe, but they make me twice as hungry. 也許，但是會讓我的肚子加倍餓。
Okay, how about an egg, fish and tomato sandwich?
好吧，那來個蛋跟魚，還有番茄的三明治如何?
Good for your body and brain. 對你的身體跟腦部都很好。

3. give 給予。
And also give you a lot of energy. 也會給你滿滿能量。

4. energy 能量。
I can sure use more energy. 我確實需要更多能量。
Great! One sandwich coming right up! 好極了!一個三明治馬上就來。

vocabulary.postscript:
你知道什麼食物很營養嗎?
來複習今天的單字。
lunch午餐 body身體 give給予 energy能量

quiz.question: 1. What does Auntie say?
options: a. You are what you see.
b. You are what you eat.
c. You are what you say.

quiz.question: 2. What does healthy food give us?
3. What do schools in Taiwan want kids to eat?
options: b. Food that is not healthy
c. Food that costs a lot of money

title: Deer Cowboys to Help Kenting

content.en: Formosan sika deer are cute. Taiwan is lucky to have them. The last wild one was seen about sixty years ago!
content.tw: 台灣很幸運，擁有可愛的梅花鹿。但是最後一隻野生的梅花鹿，生活在六十年前。之後就沒有再看見了。

content.en: About forty years ago, Kenting National Park got 22 Formosan sika deer from the Taipei Zoo. Now there are 3,600 deer in the park!
content.tw: 台北動物園四十年前給了墾丁國家公園22隻梅花鹿。現在那裏已經有3,600隻梅花鹿！

content.en: But that is too many deer for the park. They chew on trees. They eat plants. And wild dogs try to catch them. So the deer run to the road. Sometimes cars hit them…
content.tw: 但梅花鹿太多了，造成麻煩。鹿會啃樹皮、吃植物，被野狗追趕的時候會跑到馬路上，有時候還會被汽車撞到。

content.en: Park workers want 50 deer to be taken to another park this year. The other park has 20 deer!
content.tw: 公園的工作人員希望今年可以把50隻鹿送到另外一個公園，那裡現在只有20隻鹿。

content.en: But it is not easy to catch deer safely. And no one wants them to get hurt.
content.tw: 要安全地捕捉鹿並不容易，大家也不希望梅花鹿在這個過程中受傷！

content.en: So park workers are teaching some people how to catch a deer safely. These people will throw a rope around the deer's neck. They will be deer cowboys!
content.tw: 工作人員教人如何安全捕捉梅花鹿。他們會用繩索套住鹿的脖子，就像牛仔一樣！

content.en: Deer will be taken to the other park every year. This is good for Kenting National Park and the Formosan sika deer in both parks!
content.tw: 每年都會有鹿被送到另一個公園。這對墾丁國家公園和這兩個公園裡的梅花鹿都有好處！

vocabulary.preface: 墾丁公園的梅花鹿數量太多，對當地的生態和遊客都造成困惱。

content.text:
1. this year 今年。
So, are you planning another national park trip this year?
你今年打算去另外一個國家公園嗎?
Yeah! Hopefully one with fewer people this time. 是的，我希望這次人比較少。

2. too many 太多。
You went to Kenting last year, right? 你去年去墾丁，對吧?
I did… and way too many tourists there. 對，那兒的遊客實在太多了。

3. want 想要。
Maybe try one on an outlying island-like Penghu National Park.
也許你可以試試外島，例如澎湖國家公園。
I've always wanted to go fishing there! 我一直想去那裏釣魚!

4. catch 抓到。
Fishing? 釣魚? I don't know anything about that.
But sure, go catch a big one for me! 不過好啊，抓一條大的給我!

vocabulary.postscript:
你拜訪過台灣哪些國家公園?
this year今年 too many太多 want想要 catch抓到

quiz.question: 1. How many deer are in this park?
2. What are park workers teaching some people to do?
3. Where will some deer be taken to?

title: Surprise in Space

content.en: Seven astronauts were living on the International Space Station. It is four hundred kilometers above Earth!
content.tw: 有七位太空人住在距離地球大約四百公里的國際太空站。

content.en: One day, two astronauts got ready to go on a spacewalk. On spacewalks, astronauts go outside the station. They fix things or try new things.
content.tw: 有一天，兩位太空人準備做太空漫步。太空漫步就是到太空站外面修理設備或是做實驗。

content.en: But they had a bad surprise. One astronaut felt very sick! They did not go on the spacewalk.
content.tw: 但是突然發生意外! 其中一人覺得身體很不舒服，所以他們沒有進行太空漫步。

content.en: The astronauts had trained a lot on Earth. They were calm. They worked together. They knew what to do.
content.tw: 這些太空人在地球上受過很多訓練，他們知道該怎麼冷靜的合作，解決問題。

content.en: They used a machine. It looked inside the astronaut's body. They talked to doctors on Earth. The doctors wanted the astronaut to come back to Earth soon.
content.tw: 他們用機器檢查那位太空人的身體後，跟在地球的醫生聯絡。醫生說，越快回到地球越好。

content.en: People on Earth worked fast. They got a spaceship ready to go to the station. That was a big job!
content.tw: 地球上的專家趕快行動，準備一艘要飛到太空站的太空船。這可是一件大工程！

content.en: Four astronauts got ready to leave the station. Then they flew safely back to Earth on the spaceship.
content.tw: 四位太空人搭著這艘太空船，安全回到地球。

content.en: Doctors were waiting for them. And everyone was okay!
content.tw: 醫生在地球等待太空人歸來，問題也順利解決！

content.en: Astronauts train a lot. They are ready for surprises. They can help each other in space!
content.tw: 太空人受過很多訓練。他們為意外做好了準備。他們可以在太空中互相幫助！

vocabulary.preface: 在遙遠的太空站發生意外，要返回地球可不簡單。

content.text:
1. space 太空。
Hey, would you like to watch a movie tonight? 今晚想去看電影嗎?
The one about space ?那部太空電影嗎?
Yeah.對。

2. spacewalk 太空漫步。
Wonderful! I’ll go with you. 太棒了，我跟你去。
What is it about space that you like so much? 太空究竟有什麼讓你那麼喜歡?
I love seeing astronauts go on spacewalks and dodge space rocks. 我喜歡看太空人做太空漫步，躲避太空石頭。

3. calm 冷靜的。
But you don’t want to be an astronaut? 但是你不想當太空人?
No. Never. 絕對不想。
I can’t stay calm out there.我在那裏沒辦法保持冷靜。

4. what to do 怎麼做。
I wouldn’t know what to do if an accident happens. 要是意外發生，我不知道該怎麼辦。

vocabulary.postscript:
你想飛進太空嗎?來讀單字。
space太空 spacewalk太空漫步 calm冷靜的 what to do怎麼做

quiz.question: 1. Where were the astronauts living?
2. Where are spacewalks done?
3. What did people on Earth do?
options: c. Go to the space station

title: A Man and His Umbrella

content.en: A man walked down a street in Yilan. He was holding an umbrella over his head.
content.tw: 一名男子撐著傘走過宜蘭的街道。

content.en: Was it starting to rain? No, there were no clouds in the sky. Was the umbrella a sun umbrella? Well, it was a sunny day…
content.tw: 當天並沒有下雨，是個大晴天。所以那把傘是陽傘嗎?

content.en: People looked at the umbrella. Then they looked again! There was something different about this umbrella. It had a handle. It had a pole. It had ribs. But there was no cloth covering the umbrella. So it was not a sun umbrella.
content.tw: 那把傘吸引了許多人的注意。它有傘骨，有傘柄，可是上面沒有傘布! 這是怎麼回事?

content.en: The man was carrying an umbrella on his arm too. That umbrella had cloth on it. But he did not use it. He kept holding up the umbrella with no cloth.
content.tw: 男子的手臂上掛著一把有傘布的傘，卻沒有用。他撐的傘只有骨架。

content.en: One person said this umbrella was cool. Another person said maybe the man was holding up air.
content.tw: 有人覺得他的傘很酷，也有人說他撐著空氣。

content.en: No one knew why the man was holding up this umbrella. But everyone was kind. They did not laugh. They did not point a finger at him. They let him be himself.
content.tw: 大家都不懂他為什麼要用那把傘，但是沒有嘲笑他，只是默默的看著他走過去。

content.en: Why was the man holding up an umbrella with no cover? What do you think?
content.tw: 為什麼這名男子要撐著一把沒有傘布的傘呢？你覺得呢？

vocabulary.preface: 男子撐的傘，頂多只有裝飾作用。還是他在表演?

content.text:
1. street 街道。
Going out for a walk? 出去散步?
Yeah. There’s a new coffee shop down the street. 對，街上新開了一家咖啡館。
I want to check it out. 我要去看看。

2. sunny 晴朗的。
You’d better take an umbrella with you. 你最好帶把傘。
But it’s sunny outside. 可是外面有太陽。
For now. Just wait–it’ll rain. 現在有，等下就會下雨。

3. cover 遮住。
Did Google tell you that? 谷歌跟你說的?
No, my nose did. 不是，我的鼻子說的。
No worries, I’ve got a hoodie. It’ll cover me.
不擔心，我有連身帽，會遮住我。

4. carry 攜帶。
You just hate carrying an umbrella. 你就是討厭帶傘。
Exactly. 正是。
Do you take an umbrella when you go out?

vocabulary.postscript:
來一起讀今天的單字。
street街道 sunny晴朗的 cover遮住 carry攜帶

quiz.question: 1. Where was the man walking?
2. How was the weather that day?
3. How many umbrellas did the man have?

title: The Last Elephant in the Forest

content.en: In South Africa, an elephant lives in a forest. Long ago, the forest was full of elephants. Now she is the last elephant there.
content.tw: 在南非的森林裡住著一頭大象。很久以前，森林裡有很多大象，但如今只剩下她。

content.en: Elephants like to be with other elephants. People say this elephant is lonely.
content.tw: 大象是群居動物，喜歡和同伴相處。這隻大象很孤單！

content.en: Elephants can live for a long time. This elephant is about fifty years old. She might be alone for twenty more years!
content.tw: 雖然她已經五十歲了，不過大象壽命很長，這頭大象可能還要再孤單的活上二十年！

content.en: A group had a plan. They planned to bring three elephants to the forest. The cost was more than nine million Taiwan dollars. The group had this money. The three elephants were ready!
content.tw: 一個愛動物組織想幫忙，他們計畫帶三頭大象到這片森林陪伴孤單的大象，總費用超過九百萬台幣！錢和大象都準備就緒。

content.en: South Africa checked the plan. It took a long time. The group waited and waited. But the three elephants could not wait. There was another forest for them. So the group stopped the plan.
content.tw: 但是南非政府審查這個計畫花了很久時間，大家等了又等，那三隻大象等不下去了，他們去了另外一座森林，原本的計畫也叫停。

content.en: The last elephant in this forest keeps walking alone every day. But many people care about her. They hope other elephants will come to be with her one day.
content.tw: 人們好希望有一天會有其他的大象來陪她。

content.en: They hope this forest can be full of elephants again!
content.tw: 他們希望這座森林能再次充滿大象！

vocabulary.preface: 孤獨的大象需要同伴，人類似乎幫不了忙。

content.text:
1. full of 滿滿的。
Wow, this place is full of people today! 哇，今天這裡都是人!
You don't like crowds? 你是不喜歡人多?
No, I'm okay. 不會，我還好。

2. alone 單獨的。
They make me feel less alone. 他們讓我覺得沒那麼孤單。
You've got your family around you all the time! 你有家人總是圍繞著你!
Not all the time. 並沒有總是。

3. wait 等待。
Fair enough. 那倒是。
So what do you want to do here? 所以你在這兒想做什麼?
If you don't mind waiting, we can line up for that donut shop. 要是你不介意等，我們可以去排那家甜甜圈。
4. take 花時間。\nFine. But if it takes more than 30 minutes, you're buying. 好，但是假如超過三十分鐘，由你付錢。\nIt's a deal. 就這麼辦。

vocabulary.postscript: 今天的單字你記住了嗎?\nfull of滿滿的 alone單獨的 wait等待 take花時間

quiz.question: 1. How many elephants are in this forest?
2. Why did the group stop their plan?
options: a. Three elephants could not wait
b. One elephant could not wait
c. The group had no money

quiz.question: 3. What do people hope for?
options: c. A forest full of elephants

title: School Starts Every Day with Dance Party

content.en: How does your school day start?
content.tw: 你每天的上學日怎麼開始？

content.en: Most kids start their school day in a classroom. They sit at their desks. The teacher checks which kids are there.
content.tw: 大多數的小朋友一到學校會先進教室，坐在座位上等老師點名、上課。

content.en: Some kids start their school day in the school's cafeteria. They eat a healthy breakfast.
content.tw: 也有些小朋友會先去學校餐廳，吃一頓健康早餐。

content.en: At one school in the UK, kids start every school day with some dancing! Kids, parents, and staff meet in the playground. They play music. They have a fun dance party!
content.tw: 英國一所學校，小朋友每天到校後會先跳舞！學生、家長跟老師會一起到操場，放音樂，舉辦一場開心的舞會！

content.en: The school's dance parties started a few years ago. Everyone was getting ready for a big school dance. The kids were excited!
content.tw: 天天辦舞會的活動是幾年前開始的。當時，學校要辦一個大舞會，大家都很期待。

content.en: The kids wanted to show everyone their best dance moves at the dance! The staff wanted to help them practice. So they played music every morning!
content.tw: 學生想在舞會上表現最棒的舞步，老師也想幫他們練習，所以每天早上都會放音樂讓大家跳舞。

content.en: Everyone loved dancing in the morning. Parents and staff danced too! The principal saw everyone loved the dance parties. So the music and dancing stayed!
content.tw: 大家都喜歡早上跳舞，連家長跟老師也來加入！校長看到了，就把這個活動保留下來。

content.en: Dancing is a kind of exercise. It gets us moving. It’s good for our bodies. It gives us lots of energy!
content.tw: 跳舞要全身都動起來，是很棒的運動，讓我們充滿活力！

content.en: Try it! Start your day by dancing, and see how good you feel!
content.tw: 試試看吧！用跳舞來開啟你的一天，看看你的感覺有多棒！

vocabulary.preface: 跳舞讓人快樂，英國這所小學的學生每天都跳舞。

content.text:
1. every morning 每天早上。
Do you like dancing? 你喜不喜歡跳舞?
I do, but these days, it’s mostly at the gym. 喜歡，不過現在都是在健身房跳。
I dance in the park every morning! 我是每天早上在公園裡跳。

2. move 動作。
Oh, I’ve seen that! 我有看過。
Line dancing, right? 排舞，對吧?
Everyone doing the same moves? 大家都做一樣的動作?
That’s the one. 就是那個。

3. music 音樂。
Come join us sometime. 你找時間來加入吧。
We dance to great music! 我們跟著很棒的音樂跳!

4. playground 遊戲場。
Where do you do it? 在哪裡?
On the big playground over there—hard to miss! 就在那裏的大遊戲場，很難找不到。

vocabulary.postscript:
來跳舞吧!
every morning每天早上 move動作 music音樂 playground遊戲場

quiz.question: 1. Where do most kids start their school day?
2. When does the UK school have a fun dance party?
3. What does dancing give us lots of?

title: Company Says Its Robots Will Do Our Work

content.en: A company makes cars. But now it wants to make robots! These robots have a head, two arms, and two legs. They look a little like humans. They move like humans.
content.tw: 一家汽車製造公司想做機器人，有頭，有四肢，看起來像人，動作也像人。

content.en: The company wants to make one million robots every year. It says the robots will do lots of things. They will clean our homes. They will do work for companies.
content.tw: 這家公司準備每年製造一百萬個機器人，會打掃家裡，也會在公司工作。

content.en: The company's boss thinks everyone will want a robot. He says one robot will cost from $640,000 Taiwan dollars to one million Taiwan dollars.
content.tw: 公司老闆相信大家都想要機器人，每個的售價從六十四萬台幣到一百萬不等。

content.en: The company's boss also says robots will help people a lot. He says people will not have to work! He says robots will be able to do all the work.
content.tw: 他說有了機器人，人類就不必親自工作了，機器人可以代勞。

content.en: But people can do many things. So some people say robots will not be able to do everything people can. Robots can do some things now. They can serve popcorn. They can play ping pong. But will they be able to do everyone's job?
content.tw: 但是機器人真的有那麼能幹，會做所有的事情嗎? 他現在可以做爆米花，可以打桌球，但是接替人類的工作，行嗎?

content.en: What do you think? Will a robot be able to do everything for you one day?
content.tw: 你覺得呢？有一天機器人能為你做所有的事情嗎？

vocabulary.preface: 一家公司想要製造許多機器人，協助人類做大部分的工作。

content.text:
1. play 打球。
If you had a robot, what would you have it do?
假如你有個機器人，你會要它做哪些事?
Hmmmm, play tennis with me every night, 每天晚上陪我打網球，
and let me win! 並且讓我贏!

2. have to 必須。
And it has to look like a human, 當 然，而且要它看起來很像人，
with arms and legs…and a friendly face. 有手臂跟腿，跟一張友善的臉。

3. clean 清潔打掃。
What else do you want it to do? 你還想要它做什麼?
Clean my home and tell me jokes. 打掃我家，講笑話給我聽。

4. say 說話。
What about you? 你呢?
I just want it to follow me around saying, "You're doing great!"
我只要它到處跟著我，告訴我: [你做得很棒!]
Do you need a robot?

vocabulary.postscript:
來複習單字。
play打球 have to必須 clean清潔打掃 say說話

quiz.question: 1. What do the company's robots look a little like?
2. What does the company's boss say people will not have to do?
3. What can the company's robots do now?

title: Stinky Tofu on a Mountain in Keelung

content.en: On a mountain in Keelung, there is a stinky tofu shop. One man runs it. His name is A-hsing. Many trees are around the small shop. It has lots of signs outside. A-hsing wrote them.
content.tw: 基隆山上有一家臭豆腐店，老闆兼廚師，名叫阿興，他的小店外面有很多樹叢，大大的標誌都是他手寫的。

content.en: Eating at this shop is not easy. First, people have to shout the password outside. Can you guess it?
content.tw: 來這裡吃臭豆腐需要講通關密語，才能吃到。是什麼密語你猜到了嗎?

content.en: The password is "A-hsing! I want stinky tofu!" You have to shout this a few times. If you do not, maybe A-hsing will not come out!
content.tw: 沒錯，就是:[阿興，我要吃臭豆腐]，而且要大聲叫好幾次，否則老闆可能不會出現。

content.en: The shop has many stinky tofu dishes. Some are fried and crispy. Others are soft like pudding.
content.tw: 這家店有多種不同的臭豆腐吃法，有的是油炸的酥脆口感，有的滑嫩像布丁。

content.en: You can choose how stinky your tofu is. There is stinky, very stinky, or extra stinky tofu!
content.tw: 連臭味也可以選，中臭，大臭，或是特別臭!

content.en: Some people think eating stinky tofu on a mountain is strange. But some drive a long way to eat it! People say it smells strong. But it is fun to try! Woohoo!
content.tw: 有人覺得把店開在偏僻的山上很奇怪，也有人願意大老遠專程來吃。有人說味道實在太強烈，但它是有趣的經驗。

content.en: If you eat this stinky tofu in the city, maybe your neighbors will not like the smell. But on this mountain in Keelung, A-hsing's tofu is a stinky star!
content.tw: 如果你在城市裡吃這種臭豆腐，你的鄰居可能不會喜歡那個氣味。但在基隆的這座山上，阿興的臭豆腐是個大明星！

vocabulary.preface: 這家基隆臭豆腐店遠近馳名，去吃一趟可不容易。

content.text:
1. smell 氣味。
What are you cooking? 你在燒什麼菜?
The smell is… strong. 氣味好重。
Oh, it's stinky tofu. I just bought it. 是臭豆腐，我剛剛買的。

2. extra 多出來的。
It smells like something that's not from this world.
它聞起來像是來自另外一個世界。
Haha! Sorry. 真抱歉。
I added extra spices. 我多加了調味料。

3. shop 商店。
Is it from that shop you often talk about? 是你常常講的那家店買的?
Yeah, that's the one. 對，就是那家。

4. strange 奇怪的。
It's strange that people actually love it. 真奇怪有人真的喜歡。
I get it. 我懂。That's exactly how I felt when you were eating durian.
你吃榴槤時我就是那種感覺。

vocabulary.postscript:
你愛吃哪些食物呢?
smell氣味 extra多出來的 shop商店 strange奇怪的

quiz.question: 1. Where is this stinky tofu shop?
2. What do people have to do to eat at the shop?
3. What can people choose at the shop?
options: a. How stinky their tofu is
b. How long they will drive
c. How much they will pay for a dish

title: Old Vehicle Carries New Rocket

content.en: A rocket stands on a launch pad. That is where rockets take off. The rocket is 100 meters tall. NASA made it. NASA is the U.S. space agency.
content.tw: 火箭矗立在發射台上，準備發射升空。這枚火箭高達100公尺，是美國太空總署製造的。

content.en: NASA made the rocket 7 kilometers from the launch pad. So how did it get there?
content.tw: 太空總署組裝火箭的地方，距離發射台有七公里，但它是怎麼運去發射台的呢？

content.en: A vehicle carried it. Vehicles carry people and things. Cars, trucks and rockets are vehicles. The vehicle for the rocket is big. It is bigger than a baseball infield! It weighs 3 million kilograms! No other vehicle can carry the rocket. And, it is 63 years old!
content.tw: 火箭是用一台大型運輸車載過去的。這台運輸車非常大，比棒球場的內野還大，重達三百萬公斤！這台車已經有63年的歷史了，除了它以外沒有其他車能載得了火箭。

content.en: The new rocket stood on this old vehicle. The vehicle moved slowly. Many people came to watch. They took pictures.
content.tw: 全新的火箭就放在這台老車上，慢慢往前移動。很多人來觀看並且拍照記錄這一刻。

content.en: The vehicle took 12 hours to go to the launch pad! But it carried the rocket safely there. And, on April 1st, the rocket took off to go around the moon!
content.tw: 走了整整12個小時，火箭終於安全地送到發射台！在今年4月1號，火箭成功升空，飛向月球！

content.en: Many people helped make the rocket. Many people helped it take off. But the old vehicle was a big help too!
content.tw: 許多人協助製造這枚火箭，也有許多人協助它發射。但這台老運輸車也幫了大忙！

vocabulary.preface: 這枚火箭在繞月球飛行一圈之後，已經回到了地球。

content.text:
1. take off 起飛。
Come quickly! 快來!
The rocket is about to take off! 火箭要起飛了!
Wait! I want to take pictures! 等一下，我要照相!

2. moon 月球。
Wow, that's amazing! 哇，真精采!
Yeah, it's going to the moon. 它要去月球。
Will it land on the moon? 會不會降落在月球上?
Nope. 不會。

3. launch 發射。
Then why did NASA launch it? 那太空總署為什麼要發射那枚火箭?
To study the moon up close! 好近距離研究月亮啊!

4. carry 搭載。
Imagine that. 想想看。Just sitting there while a spaceship carries you into space.

vocabulary.postscript:
今天的單字你是不是都記住了?
take off起飛 moon月球 launch發射 carry搭載

quiz.question: 1. What space agency made the rocket?
2. What do vehicles do?
3. How long did it take to go to the launch pad?

title: Taylor Swift in the Songwriters Hall of Fame

content.en: Some singers do not write their songs. But pop star Taylor Swift writes her songs. She is a songwriter!
content.tw: 有些歌手不會自己作曲，但流行歌曲巨星泰勒絲，是自己寫歌的創作型歌手！

content.en: Taylor Swift writes songs about her life. She writes about her family. She writes about her friends. She writes about her boyfriends too! Her songs tell stories.
content.tw: 泰勒絲常常在歌曲中提到她的親友跟男朋友。她把自己的生活寫進歌裡，就像在說故事一樣。

content.en: The Songwriters Hall of Fame honors the best writers of songs in English. They can be honored after 20 years of songwriting.
content.tw: 「詞曲作者名人堂」專門表揚最佳的英文歌曲創作者。想進入這個名人堂，需要有20年的作曲資歷！

content.en: Taylor Swift's first album was in 2006. That was 20 years ago!
content.tw: 泰勒絲2006年發行第一張專輯，到現在剛好滿20年！

content.en: So this year, the Songwriters Hall of Fame honored Taylor Swift. She is the youngest woman to be honored!
content.tw: 今年「詞曲作者名人堂」給了她這項榮譽，她是名人堂中最年輕的女性得獎者！

content.en: This was the second time the Songwriters Hall of Fame honored Taylor Swift. In 2010, it gave her an award for being a very good young songwriter. She was 20 years old! She was the youngest woman to win that award!
content.tw: 這其實不是她第一次得獎。早在2010年，泰勒絲就拿過優秀年輕作曲家獎，當時才20歲，也是最年輕的女性得獎者！

content.en: Great job, Taylor Swift! Keep writing your songs!
content.tw: 做得好，泰勒絲！繼續創作妳的歌曲吧！

vocabulary.preface: 泰勒絲才華橫溢，能唱能作曲，年紀輕輕就獲得許多獎項。

content.text:
1. youngest 最年輕的。
Look at that girl on stage. 你看舞台上那個女孩。
Uh-hum.
She’s the youngest singer there. 她是那裏最年輕的歌手。
No kidding. She looks so calm! 真的喔，她看起來好鎮定!

2. win 贏得。
And she sings very well, too. 她也唱得非常好。
She may win the whole contest! 她可能贏得整場比賽呢!

3. song 歌曲。
Her songs are quite unusual. 她的歌滿特別的。
I know, right? 可不是?
That’s because she writes her own songs. 那是因為她的歌都自己寫。

4. write 寫作。
So she writes about memories and dreams? 所以她都寫回憶跟夢境?
She does. And so much more. 沒錯，還有很多別的。
Are you a fan of Taylor Swift? 來讀讀今天的單字。

vocabulary.postscript: youngest 最年輕的 win 贏得 song 歌曲 write 寫作

quiz.question: 1. What are people who write songs called?
2. When did Taylor Swift’s first album come out?
3. Who is the youngest woman to be honored by this Hall of Fame?

title: Bear Lives Under House

content.en: In the U.S., many black bears live in forests. But one bear lived under a house!
content.tw: 美國有很多黑熊住在森林裡。但有一頭熊竟然住在一棟房子的底下！

content.en: A big fire burned the bear's forest. There was no place for it to live there. It found a man's house. Under the house, it was dark. So the bear stayed there for more than one month!
content.tw: 一場森林大火把這頭熊原本的棲息地燒掉了，牠無處可去，最後找到這名男子的房屋。房屋底下的空間很暗，熊熊就在那裏住了一個多月！

content.en: The man did not want the bear there. It was dangerous for him and his house! This bear was about two hundred thirty kilograms. When it moved around, it broke many things!
content.tw: 房子的主人不想要熊住在那裡，牠有230公斤重，在房屋下面活動的時弄壞東西，對房屋或者是主人造成危害。

content.en: The man called for help. Some people came. They try to catch bears. They tried to make the bear come out. But the bear stayed under the house.
content.tw: 這名男子打電話求救，抓熊專家抵達，想把熊嚇出來，但是沒有成功。

content.en: Other people came. They try to help bears. One man went under the house! He went behind the bear! And the bear came out!
content.tw: 另外一群人來了。其中一人爬到房子的下面，繞到熊熊的背後，終於把熊趕出去了！

content.en: The bear came back one time. But now there is a mat where he got in before. It has electricity. Zap zap! It scared the bear away!
content.tw: 這頭熊後來還想再回來，但現在房屋的底下入口處有個通電的腳踏墊，只要一碰到就會被電，把熊嚇走！

content.en: Kids, if there is a bear under your house, do not go there! Call for help!
content.tw: 小朋友們，如果你家房子下面有一頭熊，千萬別去那裡！趕快尋求幫助！

vocabulary.preface: 這頭黑熊找到好地方棲身，卻給主人帶來麻煩。

content.text:
1. dark 黑暗的。
It's getting dark, 天越來越黑了，
and we still don't know where we are. 我們還是不知道身在何方。
You're right. We are totally lost. 你說得對，我們完全迷路了。

2. call for 請求。
Should we call for help? 我們要求救嗎?
No, let me figure it out. 不要，讓我想想辦法。

3. move around 到處活動。
Figure it out? 想辦法?
In this dark forest? 在這個黑森林裡?
Calm down. I'll move around a bit and find a way.
鎮定一點。我到處走一下，看看有沒有路。

4. break 弄斷。
Don't leave me here alone. 不要把我單獨留在這裡。
Come on, don't scare yourself. 好啦，不要自己嚇自己!
Did you hear that? 你有沒有聽到?
Something broke a branch. 有東西弄斷了樹枝。
Help!! 救命!

vocabulary.postscript:
你有迷路的經驗嗎?
dark黑暗的 call for請求 move around到處活動 break弄斷

quiz.question: 1. Why did the bear live under the man's house?
options: a. It found lots of food there.
b. A big fire burned its forest.
c. The man wanted it to live there.

quiz.question: 2. What happened when the bear moved around under the house?
options: a. People came to the house.
b. It made the man happy.

quiz.question: 3. What does the mat have?

title: Miaoli Man’s Vietnamese Tea

content.en: In Vietnam, some old trees grow high in the mountains. Some of the trees are more than one hundred years old! Going to these trees takes many hours.
content.tw: 越南的高山上，有一些歲數超過百年的大樹，想見到這些樹要花好幾個小時才到得了！

content.en: Fifteen years ago, a man from Miaoli went to Vietnam. He drank some tea. It was made from the leaves of these old trees. He loved it!
content.tw: 苗栗一名男子十五年前去到越南，喝了用這種樹的葉子泡出來的茶後，非常喜歡。

content.en: The tea was healthy to drink. But it was bitter. The local people liked it. But many other people did not.
content.tw: 這個茶對身體很好，但喝起來苦澀。當地人喜歡這種茶，其他人可一點也不。

content.en: The man wanted to make a delicious tea from these tree leaves. He wanted to take the bitter taste away.
content.tw: 這名男子想用這些樹葉做出更好喝、不會苦澀的茶！

content.en: The man's family made tea in Taiwan. He tried tea-making ways from Taiwan. He used fresh tea leaves. He used machines. He watched the weather.
content.tw: 他自己的家族就在台灣製作茶葉，因此他開始在越南使用家鄉的產茶方法。他用新鮮的茶葉，先進的機器，還觀察天氣的變化。

content.en: Making his tea delicious was very hard. Sometimes the man cried. But he did not give up.
content.tw: 過程非常困難，但是他即便他有時候累到哭，也沒有放棄！

content.en: Slowly, the tea tasted better and better. Famous people in Vietnam buy the man's tea from him now. He does not sell it in shops.
content.tw: 在他的努力之下，原本苦澀的茶變得越來越好喝！現在，很多越南的知名人士會直接來跟他買茶，商店裡還買不到喔！

content.en: Taiwan's tea-making ways helped the man in Vietnam. He made a bitter tea into a delicious tea!
content.tw: 台灣的製茶技術幫助了這名在越南的男子。他將苦澀的茶變成了美味的茶！

vocabulary.preface: 來自台灣的製茶師傅，用稀有的越南古老茶樹的樹葉，做出美味的茶。

content.text:
1. drink 喝。
Would you like some coffee? 你要來一點咖啡嗎? 
No, thank you. 不了，謝謝你。
Now I drink tea. 我現在喝茶。

2. famous 有名的。
Really? I’ve got some great tea from India. 真的哦，我有來自印度的好茶。
It’s a famous brand. 是很有名的牌子。
Nice, let’s give it a try. 真好，我們來嚐一嚐。

3. give up 放棄。
Mmmm, this is really good! 這個茶真好喝! 
It’s funny seeing you drink tea. 看你喝茶有點好笑。
Why did you give up coffee? 你怎麼放棄了咖啡? 
My sleep was getting worse. 我的睡眠越來越糟。

4. better 更好的。
And tea helps you sleep better? 茶會讓你睡得好些? 
Not really. But at least I don’t feel like a zombie.

也不會，但至少我不覺得像個殭屍。

vocabulary.postscript: Try to use these words: drink喝    famous有名的    give up放棄    better更好的

quiz.question: 1. How did the tea taste at first?
2. Where is the man from?
3. Who buys the man's tea?

title: Taiwan's Famous Puppets to Tell New Stories for Kids

content.en: Puppets are dolls that people move. Some people love puppets! They have puppet shows. They tell stories and jokes with their puppets.
content.tw: 木偶是由人來操控的娃娃。有一些藝術家很喜歡木偶，用它們演戲，講故事、說笑話。

content.en: Taiwan's most famous puppet shows are budaixi. They use glove puppets. People put the puppets on their hands. Their fingers make the puppets move.
content.tw: 台灣最有名的木偶表演叫做布袋戲。演出者把木偶戴在手上，用手指讓它們動起來。

content.en: Budaixi came to Taiwan more than 300 years ago. People went from city to city with their puppets.
content.tw: 布袋戲在三百多年前傳到台灣，戲班子帶著木偶巡迴各地表演。

content.en: Budaixi are on TV now! Budaixi TV shows have bright lights and exciting music! The puppets are beautiful! The shows are famous!
content.tw: 布袋戲現在在電視上也看得到。電視版的布袋戲有燈光和精彩音樂，木偶也做得很精緻，所以很受歡迎。

content.en: Last year, Taiwan's arts center had a budaixi story contest. It wanted stories for kids. Six stories won prizes!
content.tw: 去年台灣傳統藝術中心舉辦了一場比賽，徵求適合兒童的布袋戲，最後一共有六個作品得獎。

content.en: The first prize winner is "Giant Ali." The writer knew an Amis story about a scary monster. But in her story, the monster is kind. Another winning story is about Guishan Island in Yilan.
content.tw: 冠軍劇本叫做《巨人阿里》。作者從阿美族的怪物傳說得到靈感，但她的劇本裡面的怪物都是友善的。另一個得獎故事則是跟宜蘭的龜山島有關！

content.en: Three winning stories will be shown in Taipei in August! How about enjoying budaixi this summer?
content.tw: 三個得獎故事將於八月在台北演出！今年夏天要不要來欣賞布袋戲呢？

vocabulary.preface: 布袋戲在台灣很受歡迎，歷久不衰，現在還有給兒童看的布袋戲。

content.text:
1. puppet show 布袋戲。
Ooo, a puppet! Fancy. 一個木偶，真稀奇。
Did you make it yourself? 自己做的嗎?
Of course not. 當然不是。 My teacher made it. 我的老師做的。
We're prepping for a puppet show. 我們準備演一齣布袋戲。

2. monster 怪獸。
Nice. Do you have a name for it? 真好，你的木偶有名字嗎?
I call it Wugui, meaning turtle. 我叫它烏龜。
It looks more like a monster. 看起來更像怪獸。

3. kind 和善的。
Hey, it's a very kind monster! 是很友善的怪獸!
So what's the show about? 布袋戲在演什麼?
A super scary story! 很可怕的故事!

4. last year 去年。
You must come and watch! 你一定要來看!
I watched one last year. 我去年看過一齣。
It was really bad. 很糟糕。

vocabulary.postscript:
你有有沒有看過布袋戲?
puppet show布袋戲 monster怪獸 kind和善的 last year去年

quiz.question: 1. What kind of puppets do people put on their hands?
2. Who had a contest last year?
3. How many winning stories will be shown in Taipei this August?

title: Scientists Find New Way Some Dogs Learn Toy Names

content.en: Most dogs can learn some words like "sit," "stay," or "fetch." But for most dogs, learning the names of toys is hard.
content.tw: 大多數的狗可以學會一些簡單的指令，聽得懂[坐下]、[別動] 或[撿回來]。但是要學會玩具的名字就困難了。

content.en: Some dogs learn when they are playing with the toys. Some learn when they are learning how to do something. Dogs may learn the name "ball" when they are learning to fetch a ball.
content.tw: 有一些狗在遊戲過程中能學會玩具的名稱，有些是在訓練過程中學會的。比如說學習怎麼撿球的時候，就可能記住「球」這個玩具。

content.en: Some dogs can learn many names. Scientists found that some of these dogs can learn names another way too!
content.tw: 但是也有例外。科學家發現，有些狗用別的辦法學會很多名字。

content.en: The dog owners held a new toy. They talked about it with someone in their family. They said the name of the toy a few times.
content.tw: 狗主人手上拿著一個新玩具跟家人聊天，順便說出這個玩具的名稱好幾次。

content.en: The dogs saw the toy and heard the people talk. But the owners were not teaching the dogs the name. They did not look at the dogs. They did not talk to the dogs! The dogs listened and learned!
content.tw: 主人不需要教狗狗，也不用看著牠們，或是對牠們說話。狗只是看到玩具，聽到人在討論，就能學會！

content.en: A few days later, the owners said the toy name. The dogs fetched the toy!
content.tw: 幾天後，主人只要說出玩具的名稱，狗就能把它拿過來！

content.en: Scientists want to know why these dogs are very good learners. But these dogs are hard to find.
content.tw: 科學家好奇這些狗為什麼學習能力那麼強，但其實這些狗很稀有。

content.en: Does your dog know many toy names?
content.tw: 你的狗狗知道很多玩具的名字嗎？

vocabulary.preface: 聰明的狗狗可以用不同的方式，學會玩具的名稱。

content.text:
1. toy 玩具。
My boss told me not to play with my toy car during the meeting. 我的老闆叫我開會不要玩小汽車。 What? Did he say why? 什麼，他有沒有解釋? Yeah.有。

2. talk 談話。
He said, "You are not five." 他說[你又不是五歲]。 Fair point…so why were you doing it? 有道理。那你為什麼玩? Because talking to him is stressful! 因為跟他講話壓力很大!

3. listen 注意聽。
My toy car helped me listen to what he's saying. 小汽車幫我注意聽他的講話內容。 Then you should tell him that! 那你告訴他啊!

4. learn 學習。
No. Maybe I can learn another way to focus. 不了。也許我可以學會用別的辦法加強注意。 Good! That's a smart choice. 很好，聰明的選擇。

vocabulary.postscript:
你常常玩哪一種玩具?
toy玩具 talk談話 listen注意聽 learn學習

quiz.question: 1. What is hard to learn for most dogs?
2. What did scientists find out?
options: a. Dogs learn faster than their owners.
b. Some dogs listen and learn names.
c. Words like "stay" are easy to learn.

quiz.question: 3. What do scientists want to know about dogs that are good learners?
options: a. Why they are good learners
b. Why they are hard to find

title: Wasabi on the Alishan Mountains

content.en: Have you tried wasabi? It is a spicy green paste. The paste comes from the root of wasabi plants. Wasabi plants grow in humid, cold places. They can take two years to grow!
content.tw: 你吃過芥末嗎？芥末是一種綠色的、很嗆的調味醬，它是用山葵的根做出來的。山葵喜歡濕冷的環境，生長期長達兩年！

content.en: The Tsou people farmed wasabi. They grew it high on the Alishan mountains. But the land was Taiwan's. A forest was there before.
content.tw: 鄒族人以前會在阿里山很高的山上種山葵。不過，那塊地更早是森林，而且是公有地。

content.en: Nine years ago, Taiwan stopped letting farmers grow wasabi there. It wanted the forest and wild animals to come back. Soon, young Tsou farmers left Alishan. They went to work in cities.
content.tw: 九年前，政府為了復育森林跟野生動物，不再讓原住民在那裡種山葵。很多年輕的鄒族農民於是離開了阿里山，到城市工作。

content.en: But now some Tsou people are farming wasabi again! The new farms are lower on the mountains. The air is not as cold. There are more insects. The Tsou people work with scientists. They found a new kind of wasabi plant. The paste is sweet and spicy. Chefs tried it. They liked it!
content.tw: 現在，鄒族人又開始種植山葵了。但是新的地點比較靠近山腳，天氣不夠涼冷，又有很多昆蟲！鄒族人跟科學家合作，找到新的山葵品種，做出來的芥末香甜辛辣，受到很多廚師喜愛！

content.en: Now the forest and wild animals are back. The Tsou people are farming wasabi again. This is a win-win for Taiwan and the Tsou people!
content.tw: 現在森林與野生動物都回來了。鄒族人又開始種植山葵。這對台灣與鄒族人來說是雙贏的局面！

vocabulary.preface: 芥末來自山葵的根莖，現在台灣也有在阿里山上栽培的新鮮山葵。

content.text:
1. city 城市。
I'm done with the city. 我受夠城市了!
I want to be a farmer. 我想去當農夫。
You? A farmer? 你當農夫?
You can't live a day without your air-conditioner! 沒有冷氣你一天都活不下去!

2. mountain 山。
That's because it's too hot. 那是因為天氣太熱。
I'll go somewhere cold. 我會去很冷的地方。
In the mountains? 在山上嗎?
Yeah, somewhere nice and beautiful. 對，在很美的地方。

3. grow 種植。
And grow… peaches? 然後種水蜜桃?
Nah, too much trouble. 不，太麻煩了。
I'll grow radishes.我種蘿蔔。
You don't even like radishes! 你甚至不愛蘿蔔!

4. plant 種植。
That's just the beginning. 那只是開始。
I'll plant roses later on. 之後我要種玫瑰花。

vocabulary.postscript:
還記得剛剛的單字嗎?
city城市 mountain山 grow種植 plant種植

quiz.question: 1. Where does wasabi grow?
2. How long do wasabi plants take to grow?
3. Why did Taiwan stop letting farmers grow wasabi high in the mountains?
options: a. To make a wild animal zoo
b. To make a road to the top
c. To make the forest come back

title: Kigali Is Bringing Back Its Wetlands

content.en: Wetlands are land covered in water. Taiwan has many wetlands. Go for a walk in one. You will lose your boots in the mud! Wetlands are full of life! Many animals and plants live there.
content.tw: 濕地是被水覆蓋的土地，有很多動物跟植物住在那裡！走在濕地上，只要一不小心鞋子就可能會陷在泥巴裡！

content.en: Wetlands keep us safe. When it rains, wetlands take in water. This helps stop floods. But climate change is hurting wetlands. It is changing when rain comes and how much rain falls. It is bringing warmer weather. About one-fourth of the world's wetlands are gone. Another one-fourth are very sick.
content.tw: 濕地能夠保護我們。下雨的時候雨水會被濕地吸收！但是，氣候變遷正在傷害濕地，降雨的時間跟雨量也都在改變。現在全世界大約四分之一的濕地已經消失，另外四分之一狀況不佳。

content.en: Kigali is a city in Rwanda. It has heavy rain and lots of floods. Floods destroy homes and businesses. They hurt people.
content.tw: 非洲國家盧安達的基加利市常常下大雨，也很容易發生洪水。洪水破壞房屋跟商店，也會傷害居民。

content.en: Kigali had many wetlands. But they were used for fishing and farming. Now, people there want to bring the wetlands back! They are building islands, lakes, and ponds. They are planting plants. They are making rivers!
content.tw: 以前基加利有很多濕地，但都變成漁業跟農業用地。現在當地人想要濕地回來。他們建造小島、挖掘湖泊跟池塘，並且栽種植物，讓河流重新出現！

content.en: One of Kigali's wetlands has been saved! It has water, life, and a forest! It helps a lot with floods. Five more wetlands will be saved this year!
content.tw: 其中一個濕地已經被救回來了！那裡有水、有生命、有森林，能有效減少洪水的發生。基加利預計今年還會再恢復五個濕地！

content.en: Wetlands save lives. Let's all keep our wetlands safe!
content.tw: 濕地拯救生命。讓我們大家一起保護我們的濕地吧！

vocabulary.preface: 在非洲的基加利市，政府和人民合作搶救濕地。

content.text:
o island 島嶼。
Going birdwatching next weekend? Yeah, this time we're heading to the Matsu islands.

o water 水。
Aren't those just tiny islands? Small, yeah, but packed with water birds.

o full 充滿的。
I don't get it. What's so interesting about birds? Just wait till you see them flying over the islands; the whole place is so full of life!

o rain 下雨。
Okay, that does sound cool. And after a little rain, the birds will look for food. It's cute!

vocabulary.postscript: 我們一起讀單字。island島嶼 water水 full充滿的 rain下雨

quiz.question: 1. What are wetlands?
2. What is hurting wetlands?
3. Where is Kigali?
